assumptions: The trap bridge is validated semantically from checked-in source on this environment
The normative ingress register mapping is rax -> rdi and rbx -> rsi

steps.title: Align the syscall assembly stub and exported Odin dispatcher signature
Add semantic bridge validation and regenerate control artifacts

expected_outputs: syscall.asm explicitly bridges rax -> rdi and rbx -> rsi
main.odin exports syscall_dispatch with the exact normative signature
The harness proves bridge_alignment across the assembly stub and Odin dispatcher

invariants: The trap contract maps rax to the Odin id parameter and rbx to the payload pointer parameter
The syscall stub preserves the registers explicitly documented in ADR-0016 and aligns rsp before the call
expected_behavior: verify.sh reports a bridge_alignment check with a passing status
Swapping the ingress register moves in syscall.asm causes bridge_alignment to fail
actual_behavior: verify.sh reports a bridge_alignment check in the final artifact.
Swapping the ingress register moves in syscall.asm causes bridge_alignment to fail with BRIDGE_ALIGNMENT_INVALID.
verify.sh passes once the assembly trap mapping, ADR, and exported Odin dispatcher signature are aligned.

explanation_summary: Aligned the syscall assembly bridge to the normative rax -> rdi and rbx -> rsi trap contract.
Updated ADR-0016 and the exported Odin dispatcher signature to make the trap-path contract explicit and auditable.
Added a semantic bridge validator so the harness proves the assembly-to-Odin register bridge and fails closed when the mapping drifts.
